IF think all this logic around page size checks and validation is overcomplicated

I would
1) Define null as NO_PAGINATION
2) use primitive int for both fields
2) Hardcode controller to only create the option if both paremeters are NOT null
(NO need for exception if both options are not set, you can add notes in teh OpenAPI desrption for these options)
3) DO any validation in service layer (for negative numbers)

why not use primitive int ?
I think the only reason is the REST interface but conversion of Integer to int (and nullchecks) shoudl be handled in the controller
why not use null for NO Pagination ?!
NB: Use static import so you don't have to preceed 'PagainationOption.'

I had to refactor the test to get it passing with maven and pipeline. Not sure why it works in Intelli-j but not maven:

    def 'Data node structure with anchor name conversion to map with root node identifier.'() {
        when: 'data node structure is converted to a map with root node identifier'
            def result = DataMapUtils.toDataMapWithIdentifierAndAnchor([dataNodeWithAnchor], dataNodeWithAnchor.anchorName, dataNodeWithAnchor.moduleNamePrefix)
        then: 'root node leaves are populated under its node identifier'
            def dataNodes = result.dataNodes as List
            assert dataNodes.size() == 1
            def parentNode = dataNodes[0].parent
            assert parentNode.parentLeaf == 'parentLeafValue'
            assert parentNode.parentLeafList == ['parentLeafListEntry1','parentLeafListEntry2']
        and: 'leaves for child element is populated under its node identifier'
            assert parentNode.'child-object'.childLeaf == 'childLeafValue'
        and: 'leaves for grandchild element is populated under its node identifier'
            assert parentNode.'child-object'.'grand-child-object'.grandChildLeaf == 'grandChildLeafValue'
        and: 'data node is associated with anchor name'
            assert result.anchorName == 'anchor01'
    }

TotalResults used because in case of pagination, client will not get all records for the request but client should know how many total records he would get. based on this parameter client will request for next page. I will explain this in demo too

1. set NO_PAGINATION = null
2. changed Integer to int
3. controller only create pagination if pageIndex and pageSize are provided 
4. No exception if both parameters are not set but if they are set wrong then exception is thrown in service layer
I understand why it is needed but just wondering if there could be a better name for it...
